           Branch data     Line data    Source code
       1                 :            : /*-------------------------------------------------------------------------
       2                 :            :  *
       3                 :            :  * plancache.c
       4                 :            :  *        Plan cache management.
       5                 :            :  *
       6                 :            :  * We can store a cached plan in either fully-planned format, or just
       7                 :            :  * parsed-and-rewritten if the caller wishes to postpone planning until
       8                 :            :  * actual parameter values are available.  CachedPlanSource has the same
       9                 :            :  * contents either way, but CachedPlan contains a list of PlannedStmts
      10                 :            :  * and bare utility statements in the first case, or a list of Query nodes
      11                 :            :  * in the second case.
      12                 :            :  *
      13                 :            :  * The plan cache manager itself is principally responsible for tracking
      14                 :            :  * whether cached plans should be invalidated because of schema changes in
      15                 :            :  * the objects they depend on.  When (and if) the next demand for a cached
      16                 :            :  * plan occurs, the query will be replanned.  Note that this could result
      17                 :            :  * in an error, for example if a column referenced by the query is no
      18                 :            :  * longer present.      The creator of a cached plan can specify whether it
      19                 :            :  * is allowable for the query to change output tupdesc on replan (this
      20                 :            :  * could happen with "SELECT *" for example) --- if so, it's up to the
      21                 :            :  * caller to notice changes and cope with them.
      22                 :            :  *
      23                 :            :  * Currently, we track exactly the dependencies of plans on relations and
      24                 :            :  * user-defined functions.  On relcache invalidation events or pg_proc
      25                 :            :  * syscache invalidation events, we invalidate just those plans that depend
      26                 :            :  * on the particular object being modified.  (Note: this scheme assumes
      27                 :            :  * that any table modification that requires replanning will generate a
      28                 :            :  * relcache inval event.)  We also watch for inval events on certain other
      29                 :            :  * system catalogs, such as pg_namespace; but for them, our response is
      30                 :            :  * just to invalidate all plans.  We expect updates on those catalogs to
      31                 :            :  * be infrequent enough that more-detailed tracking is not worth the effort.
      32                 :            :  *
      33                 :            :  *
      34                 :            :  * Portions Copyright (c) 1996-2008, PostgreSQL Global Development Group
      35                 :            :  * Portions Copyright (c) 1994, Regents of the University of California
      36                 :            :  *
      37                 :            :  * IDENTIFICATION
      38                 :            :  *        $PostgreSQL: pgsql/src/backend/utils/cache/plancache.c,v 1.23 2008/10/04 21:56:54 tgl Exp $
      39                 :            :  *
      40                 :            :  *-------------------------------------------------------------------------
      41                 :            :  */
      42                 :            : #include "postgres.h"
      43                 :            : 
      44                 :            : #include "utils/plancache.h"
      45                 :            : #include "access/transam.h"
      46                 :            : #include "catalog/namespace.h"

      51                 :            : #include "optimizer/planmain.h"
      52                 :            : #include "storage/lmgr.h"
      53                 :            : #include "tcop/pquery.h"
      54                 :            : #include "tcop/tcopprot.h"
      55                 :            : #include "tcop/utility.h"
      56                 :            : #include "utils/inval.h"
      57                 :            : #include "utils/memutils.h"

      77                 :            : /*
      78                 :            :  * InitPlanCache: initialize module during InitPostgres.
      79                 :            :  *
      80                 :            :  * All we need to do is hook into inval.c's callback lists.
      81                 :            :  */
      82                 :            : void
      83                 :            : InitPlanCache(void)
      84                 :            : {
      85                 :          0 :         CacheRegisterRelcacheCallback(PlanCacheRelCallback, (Datum) 0);
      86                 :          0 :         CacheRegisterSyscacheCallback(PROCOID, PlanCacheFuncCallback, (Datum) 0);
      87                 :          0 :         CacheRegisterSyscacheCallback(NAMESPACEOID, PlanCacheSysCallback, (Datum) 0);
      88                 :          0 :         CacheRegisterSyscacheCallback(OPEROID, PlanCacheSysCallback, (Datum) 0);
      89                 :          0 :         CacheRegisterSyscacheCallback(AMOPOPID, PlanCacheSysCallback, (Datum) 0);
      90                 :          0 : }
      91                 :            : 
      92                 :            : /*
      93                 :            :  * CreateCachedPlan: initially create a plan cache entry.
      94                 :            :  *
      95                 :            :  * The caller must already have successfully parsed/planned the query;
      96                 :            :  * about all that we do here is copy it into permanent storage.
      97                 :            :  *
      98                 :            :  * raw_parse_tree: output of raw_parser()
      99                 :            :  * query_string: original query text (can be NULL if not available, but
     100                 :            :  *              that is discouraged because it degrades error message quality)
     101                 :            :  * commandTag: compile-time-constant tag for query, or NULL if empty query
     102                 :            :  * param_types: array of parameter type OIDs, or NULL if none
     103                 :            :  * num_params: number of parameters
     104                 :            :  * cursor_options: options bitmask that was/will be passed to planner
     105                 :            :  * stmt_list: list of PlannedStmts/utility stmts, or list of Query trees
     106                 :            :  * fully_planned: are we caching planner or rewriter output?
     107                 :            :  * fixed_result: TRUE to disallow changes in result tupdesc
     108                 :            :  */
     109                 :            : CachedPlanSource *
     110                 :            : CreateCachedPlan(Node *raw_parse_tree,
     111                 :            :                                  const char *query_string,
     112                 :            :                                  NodeTag sourceTag,
     113                 :            :                                  const char *commandTag,
     114                 :            :                                  Oid *param_types,
     115                 :            :                                  int num_params,
     116                 :            :                                  int cursor_options,
     117                 :            :                                  List *stmt_list,
     118                 :            :                                  bool fully_planned,
     119                 :            :                                  bool fixed_result)
     120                 :            : {
     121                 :            :         CachedPlanSource *plansource;
     122                 :            :         OverrideSearchPath *search_path;
     123                 :            :         MemoryContext source_context;
     124                 :            :         MemoryContext oldcxt;
     125                 :            : 
     126                 :            :         /*
     127                 :            :          * Make a dedicated memory context for the CachedPlanSource and its
     128                 :            :          * subsidiary data.  We expect it can be pretty small.
     129                 :            :          */
     130                 :          0 :         source_context = AllocSetContextCreate(CacheMemoryContext,
     131                 :            :                                                                                    "CachedPlanSource",
     132                 :            :                                                                                    ALLOCSET_SMALL_MINSIZE,
     133                 :            :                                                                                    ALLOCSET_SMALL_INITSIZE,
     134                 :            :                                                                                    ALLOCSET_SMALL_MAXSIZE);
     135                 :            : 
     136                 :            :         /*
     137                 :            :          * Fetch current search_path into new context, but do any recalculation
     138                 :            :          * work required in caller's context.
     139                 :            :          */
     140                 :          0 :         search_path = GetOverrideSearchPath(source_context);
     141                 :            : 
     142                 :            :         /*
     143                 :            :          * Create and fill the CachedPlanSource struct within the new context.
     144                 :            :          */
     145                 :          0 :         oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(source_context);
     146                 :          0 :         plansource = (CachedPlanSource *) palloc(sizeof(CachedPlanSource));
     147                 :          0 :         plansource->raw_parse_tree = copyObject(raw_parse_tree);
     148         [ #  # ]:          0 :         plansource->query_string = query_string ? pstrdup(query_string) : NULL;
     149                 :          0 :         plansource->sourceTag = sourceTag;
     150                 :          0 :         plansource->commandTag = commandTag;         /* no copying needed */
     151         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (num_params > 0)
     152                 :            :         {
     153                 :          0 :                 plansource->param_types = (Oid *) palloc(num_params * sizeof(Oid));
     154                 :          0 :                 memcpy(plansource->param_types, param_types, num_params * sizeof(Oid));
     155                 :          0 :         }
     156                 :            :         else
     157                 :          0 :                 plansource->param_types = NULL;
     158                 :          0 :         plansource->num_params = num_params;
     159                 :          0 :         plansource->cursor_options = cursor_options;
     160                 :          0 :         plansource->fully_planned = fully_planned;
     161                 :          0 :         plansource->fixed_result = fixed_result;
     162                 :          0 :         plansource->search_path = search_path;
     163                 :          0 :         plansource->generation = 0; /* StoreCachedPlan will increment */
     164                 :          0 :         plansource->resultDesc = PlanCacheComputeResultDesc(stmt_list);
     165                 :          0 :         plansource->plan = NULL;
     166                 :          0 :         plansource->context = source_context;
     167                 :          0 :         plansource->orig_plan = NULL;
     168                 :            : 
     169                 :            :         /*
     170                 :            :          * Copy the current output plans into the plancache entry.
     171                 :            :          */
     172                 :          0 :         StoreCachedPlan(plansource, stmt_list, NULL);
     173                 :            : 
     174                 :            :         /*
     175                 :            :          * Now we can add the entry to the list of cached plans.  The List nodes
     176                 :            :          * live in CacheMemoryContext.
     177                 :            :          */
     178                 :          0 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(CacheMemoryContext);
     179                 :            : 
     180                 :          0 :         cached_plans_list = lappend(cached_plans_list, plansource);
     181                 :            : 
     182                 :          0 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
     183                 :            : 
     184                 :          0 :         return plansource;
     185                 :            : }
     186                 :            : 
     187                 :            : /*
     188                 :            :  * FastCreateCachedPlan: create a plan cache entry with minimal data copying.
     189                 :            :  *
     190                 :            :  * For plans that aren't expected to live very long, the copying overhead of
     191                 :            :  * CreateCachedPlan is annoying.  We provide this variant entry point in which
     192                 :            :  * the caller has already placed all the data in a suitable memory context.
     193                 :            :  * The source data and completed plan are in the same context, since this
     194                 :            :  * avoids extra copy steps during plan construction.  If the query ever does
     195                 :            :  * need replanning, we'll generate a separate new CachedPlan at that time, but
     196                 :            :  * the CachedPlanSource and the initial CachedPlan share the caller-provided
     197                 :            :  * context and go away together when neither is needed any longer.      (Because
     198                 :            :  * the parser and planner generate extra cruft in addition to their real
     199                 :            :  * output, this approach means that the context probably contains a bunch of
     200                 :            :  * useless junk as well as the useful trees.  Hence, this method is a
     201                 :            :  * space-for-time tradeoff, which is worth making for plans expected to be
     202                 :            :  * short-lived.)
     203                 :            :  *
     204                 :            :  * raw_parse_tree, query_string, param_types, and stmt_list must reside in the
     205                 :            :  * given context, which must have adequate lifespan (recommendation: make it a
     206                 :            :  * child of CacheMemoryContext).  Otherwise the API is the same as
     207                 :            :  * CreateCachedPlan.
     208                 :            :  */
     209                 :            : CachedPlanSource *
     210                 :            : FastCreateCachedPlan(Node *raw_parse_tree,
     211                 :            :                                          char *query_string,
     212                 :            :                                          NodeTag sourceTag,
     213                 :            :                                          const char *commandTag,
     214                 :            :                                          Oid *param_types,
     215                 :            :                                          int num_params,
     216                 :            :                                          int cursor_options,
     217                 :            :                                          List *stmt_list,
     218                 :            :                                          bool fully_planned,
     219                 :            :                                          bool fixed_result,
     220                 :            :                                          MemoryContext context)
     221                 :            : {
     222                 :            :         CachedPlanSource *plansource;
     223                 :            :         OverrideSearchPath *search_path;
     224                 :            :         MemoryContext oldcxt;
     225                 :            : 
     226                 :            :         /*
     227                 :            :          * Fetch current search_path into given context, but do any recalculation
     228                 :            :          * work required in caller's context.
     229                 :            :          */
     230                 :          0 :         search_path = GetOverrideSearchPath(context);
     231                 :            : 
     232                 :            :         /*
     233                 :            :          * Create and fill the CachedPlanSource struct within the given context.
     234                 :            :          */
     235                 :          0 :         oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(context);
     236                 :          0 :         plansource = (CachedPlanSource *) palloc(sizeof(CachedPlanSource));
     237                 :          0 :         plansource->raw_parse_tree = raw_parse_tree;
     238                 :          0 :         plansource->query_string = query_string;
     239                 :          0 :         plansource->sourceTag = sourceTag;
     240                 :          0 :         plansource->commandTag = commandTag;         /* no copying needed */
     241                 :          0 :         plansource->param_types = param_types;
     242                 :          0 :         plansource->num_params = num_params;
     243                 :          0 :         plansource->cursor_options = cursor_options;
     244                 :          0 :         plansource->fully_planned = fully_planned;
     245                 :          0 :         plansource->fixed_result = fixed_result;
     246                 :          0 :         plansource->search_path = search_path;
     247                 :          0 :         plansource->generation = 0; /* StoreCachedPlan will increment */
     248                 :          0 :         plansource->resultDesc = PlanCacheComputeResultDesc(stmt_list);
     249                 :          0 :         plansource->plan = NULL;
     250                 :          0 :         plansource->context = context;
     251                 :          0 :         plansource->orig_plan = NULL;
     252                 :            : 
     253                 :            :         /*
     254                 :            :          * Store the current output plans into the plancache entry.
     255                 :            :          */
     256                 :          0 :         StoreCachedPlan(plansource, stmt_list, context);
     257                 :            : 
     258                 :            :         /*
     259                 :            :          * Since the context is owned by the CachedPlan, advance its refcount.
     260                 :            :          */
     261                 :          0 :         plansource->orig_plan = plansource->plan;
     262                 :          0 :         plansource->orig_plan->refcount++;
     263                 :            : 
     264                 :            :         /*
     265                 :            :          * Now we can add the entry to the list of cached plans.  The List nodes
     266                 :            :          * live in CacheMemoryContext.
     267                 :            :          */
     268                 :          0 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(CacheMemoryContext);
     269                 :            : 
     270                 :          0 :         cached_plans_list = lappend(cached_plans_list, plansource);
     271                 :            : 
     272                 :          0 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
     273                 :            : 
     274                 :          0 :         return plansource;
     275                 :            : }
     276                 :            : 
     277                 :            : /*
     278                 :            :  * StoreCachedPlan: store a built or rebuilt plan into a plancache entry.
     279                 :            :  *
     280                 :            :  * Common subroutine for CreateCachedPlan and RevalidateCachedPlan.
     281                 :            :  */
     282                 :            : static void
     283                 :            : StoreCachedPlan(CachedPlanSource *plansource,
     284                 :            :                                 List *stmt_list,
     285                 :            :                                 MemoryContext plan_context)
     286                 :            : {
     287                 :            :         CachedPlan *plan;
     288                 :            :         MemoryContext oldcxt;
     289                 :            : 
     290         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (plan_context == NULL)
     291                 :            :         {
     292                 :            :                 /*
     293                 :            :                  * Make a dedicated memory context for the CachedPlan and its
     294                 :            :                  * subsidiary data.  It's probably not going to be large, but just in
     295                 :            :                  * case, use the default maxsize parameter.
     296                 :            :                  */
     297                 :          0 :                 plan_context = AllocSetContextCreate(CacheMemoryContext,
     298                 :            :                                                                                          "CachedPlan",
     299                 :            :                                                                                          ALLOCSET_SMALL_MINSIZE,
     300                 :            :                                                                                          ALLOCSET_SMALL_INITSIZE,
     301                 :            :                                                                                          ALLOCSET_DEFAULT_MAXSIZE);
     302                 :            : 
     303                 :            :                 /*
     304                 :            :                  * Copy supplied data into the new context.
     305                 :            :                  */
     306                 :          0 :                 oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(plan_context);
     307                 :            : 
     308                 :          0 :                 stmt_list = (List *) copyObject(stmt_list);
     309                 :          0 :         }
     310                 :            :         else
     311                 :            :         {
     312                 :            :                 /* Assume subsidiary data is in the given context */
     313                 :          0 :                 oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(plan_context);
     314                 :            :         }
     315                 :            : 
     316                 :            :         /*
     317                 :            :          * Create and fill the CachedPlan struct within the new context.
     318                 :            :          */
     319                 :          0 :         plan = (CachedPlan *) palloc(sizeof(CachedPlan));
     320                 :          0 :         plan->stmt_list = stmt_list;
     321                 :          0 :         plan->fully_planned = plansource->fully_planned;
     322                 :          0 :         plan->dead = false;
     323 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         if (plansource->fully_planned && plan_list_is_transient(stmt_list))
     324                 :            :         {
     325 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 Assert(TransactionIdIsNormal(TransactionXmin));
     326                 :          0 :                 plan->saved_xmin = TransactionXmin;
     327                 :          0 :         }
     328                 :            :         else
     329                 :          0 :                 plan->saved_xmin = InvalidTransactionId;
     330                 :          0 :         plan->refcount = 1;                  /* for the parent's link */
     331                 :          0 :         plan->generation = ++(plansource->generation);
     332                 :          0 :         plan->context = plan_context;
     333         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (plansource->fully_planned)
     334                 :            :         {
     335                 :            :                 /* Planner already extracted dependencies, we don't have to */
     336                 :          0 :                 plan->relationOids = plan->invalItems = NIL;
     337                 :          0 :         }
     338                 :            :         else
     339                 :            :         {
     340                 :            :                 /* Use the planner machinery to extract dependencies */
     341                 :          0 :                 extract_query_dependencies(stmt_list,
     342                 :          0 :                                                                    &plan->relationOids,
     343                 :          0 :                                                                    &plan->invalItems);
     344                 :            :         }
     345                 :            : 
     346 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         Assert(plansource->plan == NULL);
     347                 :          0 :         plansource->plan = plan;
     348                 :            : 
     349                 :          0 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
     350                 :          0 : }
     351                 :            : 
     352                 :            : /*
     353                 :            :  * DropCachedPlan: destroy a cached plan.
     354                 :            :  *
     355                 :            :  * Actually this only destroys the CachedPlanSource: the referenced CachedPlan
     356                 :            :  * is released, but not destroyed until its refcount goes to zero.      That
     357                 :            :  * handles the situation where DropCachedPlan is called while the plan is
     358                 :            :  * still in use.
     359                 :            :  */
     360                 :            : void
     361                 :            : DropCachedPlan(CachedPlanSource *plansource)
     362                 :            : {
     363                 :            :         /* Validity check that we were given a CachedPlanSource */
     364 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         Assert(list_member_ptr(cached_plans_list, plansource));
     365                 :            : 
     366                 :            :         /* Remove it from the list */
     367                 :          0 :         cached_plans_list = list_delete_ptr(cached_plans_list, plansource);
     368                 :            : 
     369                 :            :         /* Decrement child CachePlan's refcount and drop if no longer needed */
     370         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (plansource->plan)
     371                 :          0 :                 ReleaseCachedPlan(plansource->plan, false);
     372                 :            : 
     373                 :            :         /*
     374                 :            :          * If CachedPlanSource has independent storage, just drop it.  Otherwise
     375                 :            :          * decrement the refcount on the CachePlan that owns the storage.
     376                 :            :          */
     377         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (plansource->orig_plan == NULL)
     378                 :            :         {
     379                 :            :                 /* Remove the CachedPlanSource and all subsidiary data */
     380                 :          0 :                 MemoryContextDelete(plansource->context);
     381                 :          0 :         }
     382                 :            :         else
     383                 :            :         {
     384 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 Assert(plansource->context == plansource->orig_plan->context);
     385                 :          0 :                 ReleaseCachedPlan(plansource->orig_plan, false);
     386                 :            :         }
     387                 :          0 : }
     388                 :            : 
     389                 :            : /*
     390                 :            :  * RevalidateCachedPlan: prepare for re-use of a previously cached plan.
     391                 :            :  *
     392                 :            :  * What we do here is re-acquire locks and rebuild the plan if necessary.
     393                 :            :  * On return, the plan is valid and we have sufficient locks to begin
     394                 :            :  * execution (or planning, if not fully_planned).
     395                 :            :  *
     396                 :            :  * On return, the refcount of the plan has been incremented; a later
     397                 :            :  * ReleaseCachedPlan() call is expected.  The refcount has been reported
     398                 :            :  * to the CurrentResourceOwner if useResOwner is true.
     399                 :            :  *
     400                 :            :  * Note: if any replanning activity is required, the caller's memory context
     401                 :            :  * is used for that work.
     402                 :            :  *
     403                 :            :  * In GPDB, this function has two extra parameters: boundParams and intoClause.
     404                 :            :  * If boundParams is set, the parameter values are used to generate the plan.
     405                 :            :  * That makes the plan specific to the given values, and cannot be reused when
     406                 :            :  * the query is executed with different params (RevalidateCachedPlanWithParams
     407                 :            :  * will mark the plan so that it will be automatically re-planned on next
     408                 :            :  * call). If 'intoClause' is given, the plan is to be used as part of a
     409                 :            :  * CREATE TABLE AS statement. That affects the distribution of the output rows:
     410                 :            :  * we cannot reuse a generic plan that fetches all the output rows into master.
     411                 :            :  * They should be distributed to the correct segments according to the
     412                 :            :  * distribution policy of the target table, instead. A non-NULL intoClause
     413                 :            :  * therefore also forces the plan to be re-planned on next call.
     414                 :            :  */
     415                 :            : CachedPlan *
     416                 :            : RevalidateCachedPlanWithParams(CachedPlanSource *plansource, bool useResOwner,
     417                 :            :                                                            ParamListInfo boundParams, IntoClause *intoClause)
     418                 :            : {
     419                 :            :         CachedPlan *plan;
     420                 :            : 
     421                 :            :         /* Validity check that we were given a CachedPlanSource */
     422 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         Assert(list_member_ptr(cached_plans_list, plansource));
     423                 :            : 
     424                 :            :         /*
     425                 :            :          * If the plan currently appears valid, acquire locks on the referenced
     426                 :            :          * objects; then check again.  We need to do it this way to cover the race
     427                 :            :          * condition that an invalidation message arrives before we get the lock.
     428                 :            :          */
     429                 :          0 :         plan = plansource->plan;
     430                 :            : 
     431                 :            :         /*
     432                 :            :          * If we are to use the parameter values in the plan, or this is a
     433                 :            :          * CREATE TABLE AS EXECUTE, we cannot re-use a generic plan.
     434                 :            :          */
     435 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         if (plan && (boundParams || intoClause))
                 [ #  # ]
     436                 :          0 :                 plan->dead = true;
     437                 :            : 
     438 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         if (plan && !plan->dead)
     439                 :            :         {
     440                 :            :                 /*
     441                 :            :                  * Plan must have positive refcount because it is referenced by
     442                 :            :                  * plansource; so no need to fear it disappears under us here.
     443                 :            :                  */
     444 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 Assert(plan->refcount > 0);
     445                 :            : 
     446         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (plan->fully_planned)
     447                 :          0 :                         AcquireExecutorLocks(plan->stmt_list, true);
     448                 :            :                 else
     449                 :          0 :                         AcquirePlannerLocks(plan->stmt_list, true);
     450                 :            : 
     451                 :            :                 /*
     452                 :            :                  * If plan was transient, check to see if TransactionXmin has
     453                 :            :                  * advanced, and if so invalidate it.
     454                 :            :                  */
     455 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (!plan->dead &&
     456         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         TransactionIdIsValid(plan->saved_xmin) &&
     457                 :          0 :                         !TransactionIdEquals(plan->saved_xmin, TransactionXmin))
     458                 :          0 :                         plan->dead = true;
     459                 :            : 
     460                 :            :                 /*
     461                 :            :                  * By now, if any invalidation has happened, the inval callback
     462                 :            :                  * functions will have marked the plan dead.
     463                 :            :                  */
     464         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (plan->dead)
     465                 :            :                 {
     466                 :            :                         /* Ooops, the race case happened.  Release useless locks. */
     467         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (plan->fully_planned)
     468                 :          0 :                                 AcquireExecutorLocks(plan->stmt_list, false);
     469                 :            :                         else
     470                 :          0 :                                 AcquirePlannerLocks(plan->stmt_list, false);
     471                 :          0 :                 }
     472                 :          0 :         }
     473                 :            : 
     474                 :            :         /*
     475                 :            :          * If plan has been invalidated, unlink it from the parent and release it.
     476                 :            :          */
     477 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         if (plan && plan->dead)
     478                 :            :         {
     479                 :          0 :                 plansource->plan = NULL;
     480                 :          0 :                 ReleaseCachedPlan(plan, false);
     481                 :          0 :                 plan = NULL;
     482                 :          0 :         }
     483                 :            : 
     484                 :            :         /*
     485                 :            :          * Build a new plan if needed.
     486                 :            :          */
     487         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (!plan)
     488                 :            :         {
     489                 :          0 :                 Snapshot        saveActiveSnapshot = ActiveSnapshot;
     490                 :            :                 List       *slist;
     491                 :            : 
     492                 :            :                 /*
     493                 :            :                  * Restore the search_path that was in use when the plan was made.
     494                 :            :                  * See comments for PushOverrideSearchPath about limitations of this.
     495                 :            :                  *
     496                 :            :                  * (XXX is there anything else we really need to restore?)
     497                 :            :                  */
     498                 :          0 :                 PushOverrideSearchPath(plansource->search_path);
     499                 :            : 
     500                 :            :                 /*
     501                 :            :                  * If a snapshot is already set (the normal case), we can just use
     502                 :            :                  * that for parsing/planning.  But if it isn't, install one.  We must
     503                 :            :                  * arrange to restore ActiveSnapshot afterward, to ensure that
     504                 :            :                  * RevalidateCachedPlan has no caller-visible effects on the
     505                 :            :                  * snapshot.  Having to replan is an unusual case, and it seems a
     506                 :            :                  * really bad idea for RevalidateCachedPlan to affect the snapshot
     507                 :            :                  * only in unusual cases.  (Besides, the snap might have been created
     508                 :            :                  * in a short-lived context.)
     509                 :            :                  */
     510         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 PG_TRY();
     511                 :            :                 {
     512                 :          0 :                         Snapshot        mySnapshot = NULL;
     513                 :            :                         TupleDesc       resultDesc;
     514                 :            :                         Node       *raw_parse_tree;
     515                 :            : 
     516         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (ActiveSnapshot == NULL)
     517                 :            :                         {
     518                 :          0 :                                 mySnapshot = CopySnapshot(GetTransactionSnapshot());
     519                 :          0 :                                 ActiveSnapshot = mySnapshot;
     520                 :          0 :                         }
     521                 :            : 
     522                 :            :                         /*
     523                 :            :                          * If this is a CREATE TABLE AS, pass information about the
     524                 :            :                          * target table's distribution key to the planner.
     525                 :            :                          */
     526         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (intoClause)
     527                 :            :                         {
     528                 :            :                                 SelectStmt *select;
     529                 :            : 
     530         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 if (!IsA(plansource->raw_parse_tree, SelectStmt))
     531         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                         ereport(ERROR,
     532                 :            :                                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
     533                 :            :                                                          errmsg("prepared statement is not a SELECT")));
     534                 :            : 
     535                 :          0 :                                 select = (SelectStmt *) copyObject(plansource->raw_parse_tree);
     536                 :            : 
     537                 :          0 :                                 select->intoClause = copyObject(intoClause);
     538                 :            : 
     539                 :          0 :                                 raw_parse_tree = (Node *) select;
     540                 :          0 :                         }
     541                 :            :                         else
     542                 :          0 :                                 raw_parse_tree = copyObject(plansource->raw_parse_tree);
     543                 :            : 
     544                 :            :                         /*
     545                 :            :                          * Run parse analysis and rule rewriting.  The parser tends to
     546                 :            :                          * scribble on its input, so we must copy the raw parse tree to
     547                 :            :                          * prevent corruption of the cache.  Note that we do not use
     548                 :            :                          * parse_analyze_varparams(), assuming that the caller never wants
     549                 :            :                          * the parameter types to change from the original values.
     550                 :            :                          */
     551                 :          0 :                         slist = pg_analyze_and_rewrite(raw_parse_tree,
     552                 :          0 :                                                                                    plansource->query_string,
     553                 :          0 :                                                                                    plansource->param_types,
     554                 :          0 :                                                                                    plansource->num_params);
     555                 :            : 
     556         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (plansource->fully_planned)
     557                 :            :                         {
     558                 :            :                                 /*
     559                 :            :                                  * Generate plans for queries.
     560                 :            :                                  *
     561                 :            :                                  * The planner may try to call SPI-using functions, which
     562                 :            :                                  * causes a problem if we're already inside one.  Rather than
     563                 :            :                                  * expect all SPI-using code to do SPI_push whenever a replan
     564                 :            :                                  * could happen, it seems best to take care of the case here.
     565                 :            :                                  */
     566                 :            :                                 bool    pushed;
     567                 :            : 
     568                 :          0 :                                 pushed = SPI_push_conditional();
     569                 :            : 
     570                 :          0 :                                 slist = pg_plan_queries(slist, plansource->cursor_options,
     571                 :          0 :                                                                                 boundParams, false);
     572                 :            : 
     573                 :          0 :                                 SPI_pop_conditional(pushed);
     574                 :          0 :                         }
     575                 :            : 
     576                 :            :                         /*
     577                 :            :                          * Check or update the result tupdesc.  XXX should we use a weaker
     578                 :            :                          * condition than equalTupleDescs() here?
     579                 :            :                          */
     580                 :          0 :                         resultDesc = PlanCacheComputeResultDesc(slist);
     581 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (resultDesc == NULL && plansource->resultDesc == NULL)
     582                 :            :                         {
     583                 :            :                                 /* OK, doesn't return tuples */
     584                 :          0 :                         }
     585         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         else if (intoClause)
     586                 :            :                         {
     587                 :            :                                 /* OK */
     588                 :          0 :                         }
     589 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                         else if (resultDesc == NULL || plansource->resultDesc == NULL ||
                 [ #  # ]
     590                 :          0 :                                          !equalTupleDescs(resultDesc, plansource->resultDesc, true))
     591                 :            :                         {
     592                 :            :                                 MemoryContext oldcxt;
     593                 :            : 
     594                 :            :                                 /* can we give a better error message? */
     595         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 if (plansource->fixed_result)
     596         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                         ereport(ERROR,
     597                 :            :                                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
     598                 :            :                                                          errmsg("cached plan must not change result type")));
     599                 :          0 :                                 oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(plansource->context);
     600         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 if (resultDesc)
     601                 :          0 :                                         resultDesc = CreateTupleDescCopy(resultDesc);
     602         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 if (plansource->resultDesc)
     603                 :          0 :                                         FreeTupleDesc(plansource->resultDesc);
     604                 :          0 :                                 plansource->resultDesc = resultDesc;
     605                 :          0 :                                 MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
     606                 :          0 :                         }
     607                 :            : 
     608                 :            :                         /* Done with snapshot */
     609         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (mySnapshot)
     610                 :          0 :                                 FreeSnapshot(mySnapshot);
     611                 :            :                 }
     612                 :          0 :                 PG_CATCH();
     613                 :            :                 {
     614                 :            :                         /* Restore global vars and propagate error */
     615                 :          0 :                         ActiveSnapshot = saveActiveSnapshot;
     616                 :          0 :                         PG_RE_THROW();
     617                 :            :                 }
     618                 :          0 :                 PG_END_TRY();
     619                 :            : 
     620                 :          0 :                 ActiveSnapshot = saveActiveSnapshot;
     621                 :            : 
     622                 :            :                 /* Now we can restore current search path */
     623                 :          0 :                 PopOverrideSearchPath();
     624                 :            : 
     625                 :            :                 /*
     626                 :            :                  * Store the plans into the plancache entry, advancing the generation
     627                 :            :                  * count.
     628                 :            :                  */
     629                 :          0 :                 StoreCachedPlan(plansource, slist, NULL);
     630                 :            : 
     631                 :          0 :                 plan = plansource->plan;
     632                 :            : 
     633                 :            :                 /*
     634                 :            :                  * If we used the parameter values to create the plan, or this is a
     635                 :            :                  * CREATE TABLE AS, we cannot re-use this plan on subsequent calls.
     636                 :            :                  */
     637 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (boundParams || intoClause)
     638                 :          0 :                         plan->saved_xmin = BootstrapTransactionId;
     639                 :          0 :         }
     640                 :            : 
     641                 :            :         /*
     642                 :            :          * Last step: flag the plan as in use by caller.
     643                 :            :          */
     644         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (useResOwner)
     645                 :          0 :                 ResourceOwnerEnlargePlanCacheRefs(CurrentResourceOwner);
     646                 :          0 :         plan->refcount++;
     647         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (useResOwner)
     648                 :          0 :                 ResourceOwnerRememberPlanCacheRef(CurrentResourceOwner, plan);
     649                 :            : 
     650                 :          0 :         return plan;
     651                 :            : }
     652                 :            : 
     653                 :            : /*
     654                 :            :  * Compatibility version of RevalidateCachedPlanWithParams, for the simple
     655                 :            :  * case of no params and no CREATE TABLE AS.
     656                 :            :  */
     657                 :            : CachedPlan *
     658                 :            : RevalidateCachedPlan(CachedPlanSource *plansource, bool useResOwner)
     659                 :            : {
     660                 :          0 :         return RevalidateCachedPlanWithParams(plansource, useResOwner, NULL, NULL);
     661                 :            : }
     662                 :            : 
     663                 :            : /*
     664                 :            :  * ReleaseCachedPlan: release active use of a cached plan.
     665                 :            :  *
     666                 :            :  * This decrements the reference count, and frees the plan if the count
     667                 :            :  * has thereby gone to zero.  If useResOwner is true, it is assumed that
     668                 :            :  * the reference count is managed by the CurrentResourceOwner.
     669                 :            :  *
     670                 :            :  * Note: useResOwner = false is used for releasing references that are in
     671                 :            :  * persistent data structures, such as the parent CachedPlanSource or a
     672                 :            :  * Portal.      Transient references should be protected by a resource owner.
     673                 :            :  */
     674                 :            : void
     675                 :            : ReleaseCachedPlan(CachedPlan *plan, bool useResOwner)
     676                 :            : {
     677         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (useResOwner)
     678                 :          0 :                 ResourceOwnerForgetPlanCacheRef(CurrentResourceOwner, plan);
     679 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         Assert(plan->refcount > 0);
     680                 :          0 :         plan->refcount--;
     681         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (plan->refcount == 0)
     682                 :          0 :                 MemoryContextDelete(plan->context);
     683                 :          0 : }
     684                 :            : 
     685                 :            : /*
     686                 :            :  * CachedPlanIsValid: test whether the plan within a CachedPlanSource is
     687                 :            :  * currently valid (that is, not marked as being in need of revalidation).
     688                 :            :  *
     689                 :            :  * This result is only trustworthy (ie, free from race conditions) if
     690                 :            :  * the caller has acquired locks on all the relations used in the plan.
     691                 :            :  */
     692                 :            : bool
     693                 :            : CachedPlanIsValid(CachedPlanSource *plansource)
     694                 :            : {
     695                 :            :         CachedPlan *plan;
     696                 :            : 
     697                 :            :         /* Validity check that we were given a CachedPlanSource */
     698 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         Assert(list_member_ptr(cached_plans_list, plansource));
     699                 :            : 
     700                 :          0 :         plan = plansource->plan;
     701 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         if (plan && !plan->dead)
     702                 :            :         {
     703                 :            :                 /*
     704                 :            :                  * Plan must have positive refcount because it is referenced by
     705                 :            :                  * plansource; so no need to fear it disappears under us here.
     706                 :            :                  */
     707 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 Assert(plan->refcount > 0);
     708                 :            : 
     709                 :            :                 /*
     710                 :            :                  * Although we don't want to acquire locks here, it still seems
     711                 :            :                  * useful to check for expiration of a transient plan.
     712                 :            :                  */
     713 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (TransactionIdIsValid(plan->saved_xmin) &&
     714                 :          0 :                         !TransactionIdEquals(plan->saved_xmin, TransactionXmin))
     715                 :          0 :                         plan->dead = true;
     716                 :            :                 else
     717                 :          0 :                         return true;
     718                 :          0 :         }
     719                 :            : 
     720                 :          0 :         return false;
     721                 :          0 : }
     722                 :            : 
     723                 :            : /*
     724                 :            :  * AcquireExecutorLocks: acquire locks needed for execution of a fully-planned
     725                 :            :  * cached plan; or release them if acquire is false.
     726                 :            :  */
     727                 :            : static void
     728                 :            : AcquireExecutorLocks(List *stmt_list, bool acquire)
     729                 :            : {
     730                 :            :         ListCell   *lc1;
     731                 :            : 
     732         [ #  # ]:          0 :         foreach(lc1, stmt_list)
     733                 :            :         {
     734                 :          0 :                 PlannedStmt *plannedstmt = (PlannedStmt *) lfirst(lc1);
     735                 :            :                 int                     rt_index;
     736                 :            :                 ListCell   *lc2;
     737                 :            : 
     738 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 Assert(!IsA(plannedstmt, Query));
     739         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (!IsA(plannedstmt, PlannedStmt))
     740                 :          0 :                         continue;                       /* Ignore utility statements */
     741                 :            : 
     742                 :          0 :                 rt_index = 0;
     743         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 foreach(lc2, plannedstmt->rtable)
     744                 :            :                 {
     745                 :          0 :                         RangeTblEntry *rte = (RangeTblEntry *) lfirst(lc2);
     746                 :            :                         LOCKMODE        lockmode;
     747                 :            : 
     748                 :          0 :                         rt_index++;
     749                 :            : 
     750         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (rte->rtekind != RTE_RELATION)
     751                 :          0 :                                 continue;
     752                 :            : 
     753                 :            :                         /*
     754                 :            :                          * Acquire the appropriate type of lock on each relation OID. Note
     755                 :            :                          * that we don't actually try to open the rel, and hence will not
     756                 :            :                          * fail if it's been dropped entirely --- we'll just transiently
     757                 :            :                          * acquire a non-conflicting lock.
     758                 :            :                          */
     759         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (list_member_int(plannedstmt->resultRelations, rt_index))
     760                 :            :                         {
     761                 :            :                                 /*
     762                 :            :                                  * RowExclusiveLock is acquired in PostgreSQL here.  Greenplum
     763                 :            :                                  * acquires ExclusiveLock to avoid distributed deadlock due to
     764                 :            :                                  * concurrent UPDATE/DELETE on the same table.  This is in
     765                 :            :                                  * parity with CdbTryOpenRelation().  Catalog tables are
     766                 :            :                                  * replicated across cluster and don't suffer from the
     767                 :            :                                  * deadlock.
     768                 :            :                                  */
     769         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 if (rte->relid > FirstNormalObjectId)
     770                 :          0 :                                         lockmode = ExclusiveLock;
     771                 :            :                                 else
     772                 :          0 :                                         lockmode = RowExclusiveLock;
     773                 :          0 :                         }
     774         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         else if (rowmark_member(plannedstmt->rowMarks, rt_index))
     775                 :          0 :                                 lockmode = RowShareLock;
     776                 :            :                         else
     777                 :          0 :                                 lockmode = AccessShareLock;
     778                 :            : 
     779         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (acquire)
     780                 :          0 :                                 LockRelationOid(rte->relid, lockmode);
     781                 :            :                         else
     782                 :          0 :                                 UnlockRelationOid(rte->relid, lockmode);
     783                 :          0 :                 }
     784                 :          0 :         }
     785                 :          0 : }
     786                 :            : 
     787                 :            : /*
     788                 :            :  * AcquirePlannerLocks: acquire locks needed for planning and execution of a
     789                 :            :  * not-fully-planned cached plan; or release them if acquire is false.
     790                 :            :  *
     791                 :            :  * Note that we don't actually try to open the relations, and hence will not
     792                 :            :  * fail if one has been dropped entirely --- we'll just transiently acquire
     793                 :            :  * a non-conflicting lock.
     794                 :            :  */
     795                 :            : static void
     796                 :            : AcquirePlannerLocks(List *stmt_list, bool acquire)
     797                 :            : {
     798                 :            :         ListCell   *lc;
     799                 :            : 
     800         [ #  # ]:          0 :         foreach(lc, stmt_list)
     801                 :            :         {
     802                 :          0 :                 Query      *query = (Query *) lfirst(lc);
     803                 :            : 
     804 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 Assert(IsA(query, Query));
     805                 :          0 :                 ScanQueryForLocks(query, acquire);
     806                 :          0 :         }
     807                 :          0 : }
     808                 :            : 
     809                 :            : /*
     810                 :            :  * ScanQueryForLocks: recursively scan one Query for AcquirePlannerLocks.
     811                 :            :  */
     812                 :            : static void
     813                 :            : ScanQueryForLocks(Query *parsetree, bool acquire)
     814                 :            : {
     815                 :            :         ListCell   *lc;
     816                 :            :         int                     rt_index;
     817                 :            : 
     818                 :            :         /*
     819                 :            :          * First, process RTEs of the current query level.
     820                 :            :          */
     821                 :          0 :         rt_index = 0;
     822         [ #  # ]:          0 :         foreach(lc, parsetree->rtable)
     823                 :            :         {
     824                 :          0 :                 RangeTblEntry *rte = (RangeTblEntry *) lfirst(lc);
     825                 :            :                 LOCKMODE        lockmode;
     826                 :            : 
     827                 :          0 :                 rt_index++;
     828      [ #  #  # ]:          0 :                 switch (rte->rtekind)
     829                 :            :                 {
     830                 :            :                         case RTE_RELATION:
     831                 :            :                                 /* Acquire or release the appropriate type of lock */
     832         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 if (rt_index == parsetree->resultRelation)
     833                 :            :                                 {
     834                 :            :                                         /*
     835                 :            :                                          * RowExclusiveLock is acquired in PostgreSQL here.
     836                 :            :                                          * Greenplum acquires ExclusiveLock to avoid distributed
     837                 :            :                                          * deadlock due to concurrent UPDATE/DELETE on the same
     838                 :            :                                          * table.  This is in parity with CdbTryOpenRelation().
     839                 :            :                                          * Catalog tables are replicated across cluster and don't
     840                 :            :                                          * suffer from the deadlock.
     841                 :            :                                          */
     842         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                         if (rte->relid > FirstNormalObjectId)
     843                 :          0 :                                                 lockmode = ExclusiveLock;
     844                 :            :                                         else
     845                 :          0 :                                                 lockmode = RowExclusiveLock;
     846                 :          0 :                                 }
     847         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 else if (rowmark_member(parsetree->rowMarks, rt_index))
     848                 :          0 :                                         lockmode = RowShareLock;
     849                 :            :                                 else
     850                 :          0 :                                         lockmode = AccessShareLock;
     851         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 if (acquire)
     852                 :          0 :                                         LockRelationOid(rte->relid, lockmode);
     853                 :            :                                 else
     854                 :          0 :                                         UnlockRelationOid(rte->relid, lockmode);
     855                 :          0 :                                 break;
     856                 :            : 
     857                 :            :                         case RTE_SUBQUERY:
     858                 :            :                                 /* Recurse into subquery-in-FROM */
     859                 :          0 :                                 ScanQueryForLocks(rte->subquery, acquire);
     860                 :          0 :                                 break;
     861                 :            : 
     862                 :            :                         default:
     863                 :            :                                 /* ignore other types of RTEs */
     864                 :          0 :                                 break;
     865                 :            :                 }
     866                 :          0 :         }
     867                 :            : 
     868                 :            :         /* Recurse into subquery-in-WITH */
     869         [ #  # ]:          0 :         foreach(lc, parsetree->cteList)
     870                 :            :         {
     871                 :          0 :                 CommonTableExpr *cte = (CommonTableExpr *) lfirst(lc);
     872                 :            : 
     873                 :          0 :                 ScanQueryForLocks((Query *) cte->ctequery, acquire);
     874                 :          0 :         }
     875                 :            : 
     876                 :            :         /*
     877                 :            :          * Recurse into sublink subqueries, too.  But we already did the ones in
     878                 :            :          * the rtable and cteList.
     879                 :            :          */
     880         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (parsetree->hasSubLinks)
     881                 :            :         {
     882                 :          0 :                 query_tree_walker(parsetree, ScanQueryWalker,
     883                 :            :                                                   (void *) &acquire,
     884                 :            :                                                   QTW_IGNORE_RC_SUBQUERIES);
     885                 :          0 :         }
     886                 :          0 : }
     887                 :            : 
     888                 :            : /*
     889                 :            :  * Walker to find sublink subqueries for ScanQueryForLocks
     890                 :            :  */
     891                 :            : static bool
     892                 :            : ScanQueryWalker(Node *node, bool *acquire)
     893                 :            : {
     894         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (node == NULL)
     895                 :          0 :                 return false;
     896         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (IsA(node, SubLink))
     897                 :            :         {
     898                 :          0 :                 SubLink    *sub = (SubLink *) node;
     899                 :            : 
     900                 :            :                 /* Do what we came for */
     901                 :          0 :                 ScanQueryForLocks((Query *) sub->subselect, *acquire);
     902                 :            :                 /* Fall through to process lefthand args of SubLink */
     903                 :          0 :         }
     904                 :            : 
     905                 :            :         /*
     906                 :            :          * Do NOT recurse into Query nodes, because ScanQueryForLocks already
     907                 :            :          * processed subselects of subselects for us.
     908                 :            :          */
     909                 :          0 :         return expression_tree_walker(node, ScanQueryWalker,
     910                 :          0 :                                                                   (void *) acquire);
     911                 :          0 : }
     912                 :            : 
     913                 :            : /*
     914                 :            :  * rowmark_member: check whether an RT index appears in a RowMarkClause list.
     915                 :            :  */
     916                 :            : static bool
     917                 :            : rowmark_member(List *rowMarks, int rt_index)
     918                 :            : {
     919                 :            :         ListCell   *l;

     953                 :            : /*
     954                 :            :  * PlanCacheComputeResultDesc: given a list of either fully-planned statements
     955                 :            :  * or Queries, determine the result tupledesc it will produce.  Returns NULL
     956                 :            :  * if the execution will not return tuples.
     957                 :            :  *
     958                 :            :  * Note: the result is created or copied into current memory context.
     959                 :            :  */

     965                 :            :         PlannedStmt *pstmt;
     966                 :            : 
     967   [ #  #  #  #  :          0 :         switch (ChoosePortalStrategy(stmt_list))
                      # ]
     968                 :            :         {
     969                 :            :                 case PORTAL_ONE_SELECT:
     970                 :          0 :                         node = (Node *) linitial(stmt_list);
     971         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (IsA(node, Query))
     972                 :            :                         {
     973                 :          0 :                                 query = (Query *) node;
     974                 :          0 :                                 return ExecCleanTypeFromTL(query->targetList, false);
     975                 :            :                         }
     976         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (IsA(node, PlannedStmt))
     977                 :            :                         {
     978                 :          0 :                                 pstmt = (PlannedStmt *) node;
     979                 :          0 :                                 return ExecCleanTypeFromTL(pstmt->planTree->targetlist, false);
     980                 :            :                         }
     981                 :            :                         /* other cases shouldn't happen, but return NULL */
     982                 :          0 :                         break;
     983                 :            : 
     984                 :            :                 case PORTAL_ONE_RETURNING:
     985                 :          0 :                         node = PortalListGetPrimaryStmt(stmt_list);
     986         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (IsA(node, Query))
     987                 :            :                         {
     988                 :          0 :                                 query = (Query *) node;
     989 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                                 Assert(query->returningList);
     990                 :          0 :                                 return ExecCleanTypeFromTL(query->returningList, false);
     991                 :            :                         }
     992         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (IsA(node, PlannedStmt))
     993                 :            :                         {
     994                 :          0 :                                 pstmt = (PlannedStmt *) node;
     995 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                                 Assert(pstmt->returningLists);
     996                 :          0 :                                 return ExecCleanTypeFromTL((List *) linitial(pstmt->returningLists), false);
     997                 :            :                         }
     998                 :            :                         /* other cases shouldn't happen, but return NULL */
     999                 :          0 :                         break;
    1000                 :            : 
    1001                 :            :                 case PORTAL_UTIL_SELECT:
    1002                 :          0 :                         node = (Node *) linitial(stmt_list);
    1003         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (IsA(node, Query))
    1004                 :            :                         {
    1005                 :          0 :                                 query = (Query *) node;
    1006 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                                 Assert(query->utilityStmt);
    1007                 :          0 :                                 return UtilityTupleDescriptor(query->utilityStmt);
    1008                 :            :                         }
    1009                 :            :                         /* else it's a bare utility statement */
    1010                 :          0 :                         return UtilityTupleDescriptor(node);
    1011                 :            : 
    1012                 :            :                 case PORTAL_MULTI_QUERY:
    1013                 :            :                         /* will not return tuples */
    1014                 :          0 :                         break;
    1015                 :            :         }
    1016                 :          0 :         return NULL;
    1017                 :          0 : }

    1019                 :            : /*
    1020                 :            :  * PlanCacheRelCallback
    1021                 :            :  *              Relcache inval callback function
    1022                 :            :  *

    1024                 :            :  * any rel at all if relid == InvalidOid.
    1025                 :            :  */

    1027                 :            : PlanCacheRelCallback(Datum arg, Oid relid)

    1030                 :            : 
    1031         [ #  # ]:          0 :         foreach(lc1, cached_plans_list)

    1035                 :            : 
    1036                 :            :                 /* No work if it's already invalidated */
    1037 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (!plan || plan->dead)
    1038                 :          0 :                         continue;
    1039         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (plan->fully_planned)
    1040                 :            :                 {
    1041                 :            :                         /* Have to check the per-PlannedStmt relid lists */

    1043                 :            :

    1047                 :            : 
    1048 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                                 Assert(!IsA(plannedstmt, Query));

    1050                 :          0 :                                         continue;       /* Ignore utility statements */
    1051 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                                 if ((relid == InvalidOid) ? plannedstmt->relationOids != NIL :
                 [ #  # ]
    1052                 :          0 :                                         list_member_oid(plannedstmt->relationOids, relid))
    1053                 :            :                                 {
    1054                 :            :                                         /* Invalidate the plan! */
    1055                 :          0 :                                         plan->dead = true;
    1056                 :          0 :                                         break;          /* out of stmt_list scan */
    1057                 :            :                                 }
    1058                 :          0 :                         }
    1059                 :          0 :                 }

    1061                 :            :                 {

                 [ #  # ]
    1064                 :          0 :                                 list_member_oid(plan->relationOids, relid))
    1065                 :          0 :                                 plan->dead = true;
    1066                 :            :                 }
    1067                 :          0 :         }
    1068                 :          0 : }

    1070                 :            : /*

    1073                 :            :  *
